matches.x: CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
BILDAD THE SHUHITE... 1. Not much know about him 2. May have been a descendent of Shuah, son of Abraham and Keturah, who lived in the "east" (Gen 25:2,6)
CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,
BILDAD THE SHUHITE... 1. Not much know about him 2. May have been a descendent of Shuah, son of Abraham and Keturah, who lived in the "east" (Gen 25:2,6)
CHAPTER VIII. Bildad showeth God's justice. PJVHEN answered Bildad: the: Shuhite, and said,

BY JOHN JAQUE8.
Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the spark of his fire shall not shine. The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him. His confidence shall be rooted out of bis tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrors.—Bildad.
In the fear of the Lord is strong confidence, and his children shall have a place of…

matches.lb: Commentary: Kitzur Ba'al HaTurim on Genesis 3:15:1
matches.x: "עקב" (heel) - both this interpretation and the other one [that עקב means "because"] are valid. [The phrase] "He will bruise your head" emphasizes [God's] punishment [of the serpent], and "you will bruise his heel" refers to the serpent's treachery. This is why our sages said [in reference to the verse in Job 18:10]: "The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way." This…

As a tree often represents a man, so a branch signifies one aspect of a man (Mal. 4:1; Job 29:19; Prov. 11:28; see also John 15:1-6). Therefore, figuratively, a man has roots, leaves, and branches. Concerning the final status of the wicked, it is said that "his roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off" (Job 18:16). (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Donald W. Parry, A…
